per_query.query: Where is the CLI argument parser defined and what subcommands does the tool support?
How does the tool handle file processing when invoked from the command line?
Where is the core logic for finding and replacing fsrc directives implemented?
How does the tool determine the language tag for code fences when auto-detecting?
How are error and status messages presented to the user in the terminal?
Where is the logic for skipping fsrc directives that appear inside backtick-fenced code blocks?
How does the line-range filtering feature work when extracting specific lines from source files?
What happens when a referenced source file cannot be read during processing?
How does the self-update mechanism detect the correct binary to download for the current platform?
How are SHA256 checksums validated when downloading a new binary during self-update?
What is the atomic replacement strategy used to ensure a safe binary update without data loss?
How does the tool ensure fenced code output is idempotent when run multiple times on the same file?
What validation does the CLI perform before attempting to process files?
How is the GitHub Actions integration configured and what inputs does it expose?
What test cases verify that the embedding process works across different file types and comment styles?
How does the tool handle the verify mode to detect if files are out of date without modifying them?
where is the command-line argument parser defined for the CLI
how are errors propagated and converted to user-facing messages
where is the embedding model downloaded and invoked for vector generation
how does the system determine which memories to surface given a query
where is the in-memory staging area for quick notes defined
how are memory files structured with frontmatter and body content
where is the main entry point and how does it dispatch to subcommands
how does the system read and index source code files into the knowledge base
where is the hierarchical navigable small world graph index implemented
how are configuration files loaded and applied across global and project scopes
how does vector quantization reduce storage footprint of embeddings
where is the live progress renderer that displays carriage-return updates on stderr
where is distance computation between vectors implemented for similarity search
how are memory consolidation rules applied during the sleep cycle
where are integration tests validating CLI output and memory operations
how does the system follow inter-layer references between code and memories
How does the CLI determine which LLM provider to use when none is explicitly specified?
Where is the agent's core invocation loop implemented and how does it manage concurrent tool execution?
How are streaming deltas from the LLM differentiated from streaming deltas produced by tool execution?
What mechanism allows the CLI to wait for user approval before executing a tool marked as requiring human-in-the-loop review?
How does the RAG pipeline handle duplicate documents and what strategies are available?
Where are the different content extraction implementations for various document formats (PDF, plaintext, etc.) registered?
How does the knowledge graph deduplication process distinguish between exact matches and fuzzy matches when ingesting entities?
Where is provider resilience (retry and fallback composition) implemented for handling transient LLM failures?
How does the conversation tree persist branching history and support rewinding to previous conversation states?
Where is the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server initialized and how are tool packs wired into the MCP runtime?
What is the interface contract for custom LLM providers and which methods must be implemented?
How does the RAG pipeline assemble context for an answer with proper citation metadata?
How does the interactive TUI runner handle streaming deltas from the agent and render them to the terminal?
How does the knowledge graph implement hybrid search combining vector similarity and full-text BM25 ranking?
How are conventional commits parsed and classified by semver bump level?
Where does the CLI argument parser define the release command with its various options?
How is the release plan computed without modifying the repository?
Where is the version bumping logic for different package ecosystems implemented?
How are release resources (tags, releases, assets, publishes) reconciled with their desired state?
What is the sequence of steps that execute during a release, and when is each one considered complete?
How does the code detect version files across different programming language ecosystems?
Where is the configuration file parsed and what is its structure?
How does the changelog get formatted and merged into the existing changelog file?
What abstraction allows the codebase to support multiple VCS providers like GitHub?
How are errors propagated and categorized throughout the release pipeline?
Where is the logic that determines if a release has already been published to registries?
How does the CLI handle configuration discovery when no explicit config file is provided?
How are workspace members (Cargo, npm, uv) discovered and used during release?
Where is the self-update mechanism that downloads and installs the latest sr binary?
How does the code stage files in a git commit and ensure idempotency across release stages?
Where is the command-line argument parser that defines the CLI interface including setup, run, update, and version commands?
How are configuration files discovered and loaded from the user's project directory?
What are the three capture backend types and how are they instantiated for different scene types?
Where are errors propagated to the user through styled console output?
How is a terminal PTY session created and its output captured as frames?
How does the web browser backend handle user interactions like clicking, hovering, and scrolling on pages?
Where is the browser abstraction defined and how does it isolate capture from the underlying browser implementation?
What is the pipeline architecture for encoding captured frames into multiple output formats concurrently?
How are terminal output streams converted to PNG images with styling and themes?
Where is the main orchestration logic that sequences scene setup, interaction execution, and output encoding?
How are web and markdown content rendered to HTML for browser capture?
How does the self-update mechanism detect the platform, fetch the latest binary from GitHub, and atomically replace the running executable?
How does the setup subcommand manage font installation across macOS, Linux, and Windows?
How does the screen capture backend detect and record a specific window or display region?
Where are GIF frames merged to reduce identical consecutive frames before encoding, and how is the resulting animated GIF assembled?
How does the system determine when a web page or terminal session has finished updating and is ready to snapshot?
